access.anon: Can query the public academic catalog and bus timetable through /api/graphql.
access.user: Has the same public read-only GraphQL surface as an anonymous caller in Phase 1.
access.admin: Has no additional GraphQL fields in Phase 1.
access.agent: Can inspect the GraphQL SDL and the empty persisted-operation manifest through authenticated MCP resources; arbitrary GraphQL execution is not exposed as an MCP tool.

rules.phase-one-read-only: Phase 1 exposes public queries only; mutations and GraphQL-specific OAuth resource handling are deferred.
rules.single-endpoint: GraphQL HTTP requests use /api/graphql with GET, POST, and OPTIONS handlers.
rules.production-discovery: GraphiQL and schema introspection are disabled in production.
rules.request-budgets: Requests are limited to 64 KiB, one operation per HTTP request, five seconds, depth 8, 10 top-level fields, 15 aliases, 10 directives, 1000 tokens, and cost 5000.
rules.pagination: Page inputs default to page 1 and pageSize 20; pageSize must be between 1 and 100.
rules.resolver-boundary: Resolvers call feature-owned services only; they do not issue internal HTTP requests or access Prisma directly.
rules.request-cache: Entity DataLoaders are request-scoped and responses are marked no-store.
rules.safe-errors: Unexpected resolver failures use Yoga error masking and must not expose internal exception details.
rules.schema-evolution: The canonical SDL snapshot is checked for drift and breaking changes in CI.

fields: Public semester, course, section, teacher, and bus queries

resources.name: graphql_operations_manifest
resources.description: Persisted-operation manifest; intentionally empty in Phase 1.
